development_status.summary: Production-ready RAG evaluation platform (v1.77.0 on PyPI, main = bc88726). Phase 1–14 complete. Active Phase 4 web overhaul + Phase 3.5 dependency line update have 10 feature branches awaiting merge into main (see development-journal.md §머지 대기). Default profile caps decisions at T2 (Evaluation Gate); T3 promote/rollback authority is owned by Reverra-Gate.
recent_changes: L-S2-data: mlflow 3.8.1 → 3.12.0, arize-phoenix 12.27.0 → 16.0.0 (rich 15+ and pandas 3 deferred — instructor 1.15.1 caps rich<15, mlflow 3.12.0 caps pandas<3)
L-S2: openai 1.40.8 → 2.38.0, instructor 1.4.1 → 1.15.1, langchain-openai 0.2.2 → 1.2.2 (src/ diff = 0; LLM prompt strings byte-identical)

W-S5-follow: Visualization.tsx error state → EmptyState + design Button + useNavigate
W-S5b: Settings.tsx surgical migration onto W-S1 design primitives
W-S6: lucide-react 0.562 → 1.16; Playwright e2e refresh (18/18 green); 3 pre-existing dead imports cleaned
W-S3: Analysis consolidation plan + alert tokenization on AnalysisCompareView/AnalysisResultView

Phase 4 W-S1~W-S5: design system + cumulative page migrations (Dashboard, EvaluationStudio, AnalysisLab, RunDetails, CompareRuns, AiSdkChat, JudgeCalibration, KnowledgeBase, VisualizationHome, CustomerReport)
active_work: P0 안정성/재현성 — profile validation, run_id 규약, DB 저장 일관성
P1 자동 회귀 게이트 — code complete; GitHub branch protection rule still to be wired by ops team
P2 멀티턴 RAG 평가 — schema + CLI loader landed
P3 GraphRAG 실험 프레임워크 — top-k vs GraphRAG A/B; v0 heuristic → v1 LLM staging
P4 Judge 캘리브레이션 Web UI — Calibration page + Playwright e2e in place
next_priorities: Merge the 10 feature branches landed this session into main and cut v1.78.0
Wire regression-gate as a required GitHub Actions check on main (operational, not code)
Bump rich to 15.x when instructor releases a version that lifts the rich<15 cap
Bump pandas to 3.x when mlflow releases a stable that lifts the pandas<3 cap

stable_surfaces.name: evalvault run
stable_surfaces.notes: Runs Ragas-based evaluation on a JSON/CSV/Excel dataset. Output stored via configured StoragePort (SQLite default). Profile selection determines LLM provider (openai/azure/ollama/vllm/anthropic).

stable_surfaces.name: evalvault regress (alias: ci-gate)
stable_surfaces.notes: Produces regression-gate JSON artifact comparing two run_ids. Exits non-zero when any metric drops by more than --fail-on-regression (default 0.05). See adapter-contract.md §regression-gate-artifact for the JSON schema.

stable_surfaces.name: regression-gate report
stable_surfaces.notes: Top-level fields: candidate_run_id, baseline_run_id, status (passed|failed), regression_detected (bool), fail_on_regression (float), test ('t-test'|'mann-whitney'), metrics (list[str]), results (list[RegressionMetricResult]), parallel (bool), concurrency (int|null). RegressionMetricResult fields: metric, baseline_score, candidate_score, diff, diff_percent, p_value, effect_size, effect_level, is_significant, regression. Never emits 'promote' or 'rollback' verdicts — that authority is T3 / Reverra-Gate.

stable_surfaces.name: evalvault history
stable_surfaces.notes: Lists stored evaluation runs (SQLite or Postgres depending on profile). Output is structured JSON when --format json.

stable_surfaces.name: evalvault export
stable_surfaces.notes: Exports a single run + metric scores as JSON. Lives under data/exports/ by convention (now gitignored as of e9c79f3).

stable_surfaces.name: evalvault analyze / analyze-compare
stable_surfaces.command_or_path: uv run evalvault analyze <run_id> [--intent ...] / evalvault analyze-compare <run_a> <run_b>
required_secrets: OPENAI_API_KEY (when LLM-driven intent analysis is enabled)
stable_surfaces.notes: DAG-based analysis pipeline. Intent catalog covers verification/comparison/analysis/improvement. Saved results addressable via /analysis/results/:id in the Web UI.

stable_surfaces.name: evalvault calibrate-judge
stable_surfaces.command_or_path: uv run evalvault calibrate-judge <run_id> --metric faithfulness --labels-source feedback
stable_surfaces.notes: Isotonic / Platt scaling for LLM judge scores against ground-truth feedback. Produces calibration artifact under reports/calibration/artifacts/<calibration_id>/. Web UI /calibration page consumes the same artifact.

stable_surfaces.name: FastAPI Web Backend
stable_surfaces.notes: REST + SSE/streaming endpoints under /api/v1/. Serves the frontend (React 19 + Vite 7 + Tailwind 4) and exposes runs, analysis, calibration, knowledge-base, and chat endpoints. Vite dev proxy at frontend/vite.config.ts maps /api → VITE_API_PROXY_TARGET (default http://localhost:8000).

stable_surfaces.name: evalvault metrics / config
stable_surfaces.command_or_path: uv run evalvault metrics; uv run evalvault config
stable_surfaces.notes: Diagnostic commands. `metrics` lists registered evaluation metrics; `config` prints the resolved configuration (no secrets in output).

verification_commands.fast: uv run pytest tests/unit/ -q -m "not requires_openai and not requires_langfuse and not requires_phoenix and not requires_mlflow"
verification_commands.closed_network: TRACKER_PROVIDER= LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY= LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY= OPENAI_API_KEY= uv run pytest tests/unit/ -q -m "not requires_openai and not requires_langfuse and not requires_phoenix and not requires_mlflow"
verification_commands.artifact_generation: uv run evalvault regress <candidate_run_id> --baseline <baseline_run_id> --format json --output reports/regression/sample.json

generated_paths: data/exports/ — local CSV/JSON exports from `evalvault export` (gitignored)
reports/regression/ — regression-gate JSON when --output is used
fixture_paths: tests/fixtures/e2e/ — curated evaluation datasets (auto_insurance_qa_korean_full.json, callcenter_summary_5cases.json, comprehensive_dataset.json, edge_cases.json, ab_test_baseline_korean.json, ab_test_candidate_korean.json, regression_baseline.json, graphrag_benchmark.json, multiturn_benchmark.json, etc.)

breaking_changes_since_last_update: L-S2 openai 1.x → 2.x — chat.completions.create API surface unchanged for EvalVault's adapters, but if an external caller of `evalvault.adapters.outbound.llm.openai_adapter` constructs the underlying `openai` client directly, they should re-verify on the openai 2.x release notes.
L-S2-data arize-phoenix 12 → 16 — Phoenix tracer init unchanged at the EvalVault adapter boundary; downstream OpenInference instrumentation pulled to 0.1.57.
new_adapter_opportunities: Closed-network smoke command: a single `evalvault check` that exercises the local SQLite + Ollama path without requiring any cloud secret. Currently the closest equivalent is the filtered unit test command in verification_commands.closed_network.
Stable JSON output mode for `evalvault history` and `evalvault config` (currently table-oriented; --format json works but field-stability guarantee is not yet documented).
Webhook/HTTP push variant of the regression-gate artifact for CI systems that want push notifications instead of polling the output file.
fields_safe_to_rely_on: evalvault CLI exit codes: 0 = success/pass, non-zero = error or regression detected
fields_experimental: RegressionGateReport.results[].effect_level (EffectSizeLevel enum string) — may add levels in future minor releases
Analysis CLI JSON shape (`evalvault analyze --output ...`) — still being refined; schema_version 0.9
Calibration artifact directory layout under reports/calibration/artifacts/ — file names stable, sub-structure beta

reusable_patterns.name: Regression gate report shape
reusable_patterns.description: Frozen dataclass with status / regression_detected / per-metric statistical comparison. Never emits T3 promote/rollback strings.
reusable_patterns.why_it_may_help_other_tools: Other source projects that produce a Tier-2 evaluation verdict can adopt the same status vocabulary (passed/failed) and per-metric statistical schema. Keeps decision-emitting code uniform across the AI Tool Suite ecosystem.

reusable_patterns.name: RAG metric aggregation
reusable_patterns.description: Per-metric pass-rate + threshold check against dataset-level thresholds defined in the dataset JSON (not env vars).
reusable_patterns.why_it_may_help_other_tools: Pattern of co-locating thresholds with fixtures (rather than env) makes regression reasoning portable across machines and CI.

reusable_patterns.name: Artifact storage layout
reusable_patterns.description: Generated artifacts under reports/<category>/<id>/, fixtures under tests/fixtures/, raw exports under data/exports/. Each gitignored separately.
reusable_patterns.why_it_may_help_other_tools: Clear category boundary prevents "is this a fixture or a generated output?" confusion in adapter writers and reviewers.

reusable_patterns.name: Local CLI invocation assumptions
reusable_patterns.description: All `evalvault` commands are non-interactive by default; --format json is available on read-paths; profile selection via --profile or EVALVAULT_PROFILE.
reusable_patterns.why_it_may_help_other_tools: Adapter wrappers can rely on stdin/stdout JSON contracts without TTY assumptions.

reusable_patterns.name: T0-T4 authority hierarchy compliance
reusable_patterns.description: EvalVault default profile caps decisions at T2 (Evaluation Gate). Never emits 'promote' or 'rollback' — those are T3 (Reverra-Gate) authority.
reusable_patterns.where_to_look: regression_gate_service.py (status vocabulary), Web UI AuthorityBadge primitive at frontend/src/design/components/AuthorityBadge.tsx
reusable_patterns.why_it_may_help_other_tools: Anti-conflation pattern: the same surface NEVER mixes a T2 verdict with a T3 string, even in error messages or UI badges.

reusable_patterns.name: Surgical UI primitive migration
reusable_patterns.description: Phase 4 W-Sx pattern — tokenize visual atoms (alert blocks, empty states, buttons) onto design primitives WITHOUT changing IA, routes, or component boundaries. Each slice ships independently with build + Playwright verify.
reusable_patterns.why_it_may_help_other_tools: Lets teams roll out a design system across an existing app without bigbang rewrites — each PR is small enough to review and revert.

known_risks: P1 regression gate code exists but enforcement depends on GitHub branch protection rule (Repo Settings → Branches → main → required check). Operators must add `regression-gate` to required checks; the EvalVault repo cannot enforce this itself.
LLM cluster transitive coupling (openai/instructor/langchain-openai/rich): cannot bump packages individually; future bump proposals must dry-run `uv lock` against the full cluster.
pandas 3.x cannot be adopted until mlflow lifts its `pandas<3` cap (mlflow 3.12.0 is current latest).
OpenAI cost: default judge is `gpt-5-mini`; profile-based fallback to ollama/vllm must be set up for closed-network runs.
manim is a dev extra and depends on Cairo system libs; `uv sync --extra dev` fails on machines without `pkg-config` + Cairo installed. Consider slimming dev extras.
Ragas 0.4.2 pin: a major Ragas bump will require RagasEvaluator changes. Defensive pin documented in pyproject.toml.
requests_to_ai_tool_suite: Adopt the RegressionGateReport schema (or a strict subset of it) when defining the cross-tool 'evaluation-gate decision' contract. Avoid mixing T2 (evaluation) and T3 (release) verdicts at the field level.
Provide a shared closed-network LLM stub (e.g., a deterministic local model) that source projects can target for verification_commands.closed_network without each project re-inventing the stub.

If the AI Tool Suite registry plans to ingest the calibration artifact directory (reports/calibration/artifacts/), please pin to schema_version >= 0.9 and document the breaking-change policy for sub-folder names.
